(born 1950) is an American [trumpeter](/wiki/Trumpet "Trumpet"), [flugelhornist](/wiki/Flugelhorn "Flugelhorn"), [arranger](/wiki/Arrangement "Arrangement") ([horns](/wiki/Horn_section "Horn section") and [strings](/wiki/String_section "String section")), [orchestrator](/wiki/Orchestration "Orchestration") and [session musician](/wiki/Session_musician "Session musician") who has played on hundreds of commercial recordings,[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) including [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson")'s *[Thriller](/wiki/Thriller_(album) "Thriller (album)")*,[[3]](#cite_note-OwsinskiIll2009-3) "[Rock with You](/wiki/Rock_with_You "Rock with You")*"*, "[Don’t Stop ‘Til You Get Enough](/wiki/Don%27t_Stop_%27Til_You_Get_Enough "Don't Stop 'Til You Get Enough")", "[Workin’ Day and Night](/wiki/Working_Day_and_Night "Working Day and Night")"[[4]](#cite_note-4) and the flugelhorn solo on [Dan Fogelberg](/wiki/Dan_Fogelberg "Dan Fogelberg")'s hit "[Longer](/wiki/Longer "Longer")". He has performed with artists such as [George Benson](/wiki/George_Benson "George Benson"), [Nik Kershaw](/wiki/Nik_Kershaw "Nik Kershaw"), [Al Jarreau](/wiki/Al_Jarreau "Al Jarreau"), [Barbra Streisand](/wiki/Barbra_Streisand "Barbra Streisand"), [Donna Summer](/wiki/Donna_Summer "Donna Summer"), [Earth, Wind & Fire](/wiki/Earth,_Wind_%26_Fire "Earth, Wind & Fire"), [Whitney Houston](/wiki/Whitney_Houston "Whitney Houston"), [Frank Sinatra](/wiki/Frank_Sinatra "Frank Sinatra"), [George Duke](/wiki/George_Duke "George Duke"), [Lionel Richie](/wiki/Lionel_Richie "Lionel Richie"), [Rufus and Chaka Khan](/wiki/Rufus_and_Chaka_Khan "Rufus and Chaka Khan"), [Natalie Cole](/wiki/Natalie_Cole "Natalie Cole"), [Aretha Franklin](/wiki/Aretha_Franklin "Aretha Franklin"), [Patti Austin](/wiki/Patti_Austin "Patti Austin"), [Toshiki Kadomatsu](/wiki/Toshiki_Kadomatsu "Toshiki Kadomatsu"), and [Yumi Matsutoya](/wiki/Yumi_Matsutoya "Yumi Matsutoya").[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)[[7]](#cite_note-:3-7)

Musical artist

**Jerry Hey** (born 1950) is an American [trumpeter](/wiki/Trumpet "Trumpet"), [flugelhornist](/wiki/Flugelhorn "Flugelhorn"), [arranger](/wiki/Arrangement "Arrangement") ([horns](/wiki/Horn_section "Horn section") and [strings](/wiki/String_section "String section")), [orchestrator](/wiki/Orchestration "Orchestration") and [session musician](/wiki/Session_musician "Session musician") who has played on hundreds of commercial recordings,[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) including [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson")'s *[Thriller](/wiki/Thriller_(album) "Thriller (album)")*,[[3]](#cite_note-OwsinskiIll2009-3) "[Rock with You](/wiki/Rock_with_You "Rock with You")*"*, "[Don’t Stop ‘Til You Get Enough](/wiki/Don%27t_Stop_%27Til_You_Get_Enough "Don't Stop 'Til You Get Enough")", "[Workin’ Day and Night](/wiki/Working_Day_and_Night "Working Day and Night")"[[4]](#cite_note-4) and the flugelhorn solo on [Dan Fogelberg](/wiki/Dan_Fogelberg "Dan Fogelberg")'s hit "[Longer](/wiki/Longer "Longer")". He has performed with artists such as [George Benson](/wiki/George_Benson "George Benson"), [Nik Kershaw](/wiki/Nik_Kershaw "Nik Kershaw"), [Al Jarreau](/wiki/Al_Jarreau "Al Jarreau"), [Barbra Streisand](/wiki/Barbra_Streisand "Barbra Streisand"), [Donna Summer](/wiki/Donna_Summer "Donna Summer"), [Earth, Wind & Fire](/wiki/Earth,_Wind_%26_Fire "Earth, Wind & Fire"), [Whitney Houston](/wiki/Whitney_Houston "Whitney Houston"), [Frank Sinatra](/wiki/Frank_Sinatra "Frank Sinatra"), [George Duke](/wiki/George_Duke "George Duke"), [Lionel Richie](/wiki/Lionel_Richie "Lionel Richie"), [Rufus and Chaka Khan](/wiki/Rufus_and_Chaka_Khan "Rufus and Chaka Khan"), [Natalie Cole](/wiki/Natalie_Cole "Natalie Cole"), [Aretha Franklin](/wiki/Aretha_Franklin "Aretha Franklin"), [Patti Austin](/wiki/Patti_Austin "Patti Austin"), [Toshiki Kadomatsu](/wiki/Toshiki_Kadomatsu "Toshiki Kadomatsu"), and [Yumi Matsutoya](/wiki/Yumi_Matsutoya "Yumi Matsutoya").[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)[[7]](#cite_note-:3-7)

He is known as the trumpeter and arranger for [Seawind](/wiki/Seawind_(band) "Seawind (band)"), whose other members have included [Gary Grant](/wiki/Gary_Grant_(musician) "Gary Grant (musician)"), [Larry Williams](/wiki/Larry_Williams_(jazz_musician) "Larry Williams (jazz musician)") and [Bill Reichenbach Jr.](/wiki/Bill_Reichenbach_Jr. "Bill Reichenbach Jr.")

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jerry_Hey&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Jerry Hey was born in 1950 in Dixon, Illinois, to a family of musicians. His mother was a pianist and his father was a trombonist; two older brothers played trombone and tuba. After completing high school, Hey attended the [National Music Camp](/wiki/National_Music_Camp "National Music Camp") for two summers. While in college, Hey studied under [Bill Adam](/wiki/William_Adam_(trumpeter) "William Adam (trumpeter)") at Indiana University.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[7]](#cite_note-:3-7)

Hey then relocated to [Hawaii](/wiki/Hawaii "Hawaii") to become a member of the band [Seawind](/wiki/Seawind_(band) "Seawind (band)").[[9]](#cite_note-:1-9)[[7]](#cite_note-:3-7)

In 1976, Hey moved to Los Angeles with Seawind, which then recorded two albums for [CTI Records](/wiki/CTI_Records "CTI Records") under the direction of [Harvey Mason](/wiki/Harvey_Mason "Harvey Mason").[[9]](#cite_note-:1-9)[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)

Gary Grant, who had already been in the city for a year when Hey arrived and was a well-known session player, invited Hey to join him on recording sessions, which helped to launch his career as a studio musician.[[9]](#cite_note-:1-9)[[7]](#cite_note-:3-7)

Soon after arriving in LA, Quincy Jones got in touch with Hey to ask him to play and arrange for Jones's album *[I Heard That!!](/wiki/I_Heard_That!! "I Heard That!!")*. Following that session, Hey and his associates were invited to perform on every one of Quincy's recordings.[[9]](#cite_note-:1-9)[[7]](#cite_note-:3-7) Hey later worked as a musician and arranger with [David Foster](/wiki/David_Foster "David Foster").[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Hey is the uncle of American keyboardist, songwriter, producer, arranger and musical director [Henry Hey](/wiki/Henry_Hey "Henry Hey").

He composed and arranged the song "[Jedi Rocks](/wiki/Jedi_Rocks "Jedi Rocks")" for the 1997 Special Edition re-release of *[Return of the Jedi](/wiki/Return_of_the_Jedi "Return of the Jedi")*. He co-produced four songs on [Lisa Stansfield](/wiki/Lisa_Stansfield "Lisa Stansfield")'s 2014 album *[Seven](/wiki/Seven_(Lisa_Stansfield_album) "Seven (Lisa Stansfield album)")*.

Hey has received 6 [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards") and 11 nominations.[[11]](#cite_note-11)
